message:
fix(stdio): drop _IO_2_1_stdin_/stdout_/stderr_ pointer aliases that ABI-lie about FILE storage

The release build of frankenlibc-abi was exporting linker aliases:

    _IO_2_1_stdin_  -> stdin
    _IO_2_1_stdout_ -> stdout
    _IO_2_1_stderr_ -> stderr

via global_asm! `.set` directives plus extern "C" mirror declarations,
and was listing those three names as exported symbols in the libc.map
version script under GLIBC_2.2.5. The init_host_stdio_streams hook also
copy-relocated `_IO_2_1_stdin_/stdout_/stderr_` whenever it patched
`stdin/stdout/stderr` so both sets of cells stayed point-equal.

That is an ABI lie. In real glibc, `stdin / stdout / stderr` are
*pointer cells* (`FILE * volatile stdin`) but `_IO_2_1_stdin_ /
_IO_2_1_stdout_ / _IO_2_1_stderr_` are the actual `_IO_FILE_plus`
*objects* the pointer cells point to. Aliasing the two means a glibc
consumer that resolves `_IO_2_1_stdin_` directly (the dynamic loader
and parts of libc startup do this) ends up reading the first 8 bytes
of an `_IO_FILE_plus` header out of what is really an 8-byte pointer
value. Under preload, that is enough to corrupt the very early-startup
stdio handshake and surface as "ld.so segfault before main."

This commit removes the lie:

- Strip the `cfg(not(debug_assertions))` extern "C" mirror
  declarations and the `global_asm!` `.set _IO_2_1_stdin_, stdin` /
  `_IO_2_1_stdout_, stdout` / `_IO_2_1_stderr_, stderr` block from
  stdio_abi.rs entirely.
- Drop the matching `_IO_2_1_stdin_; _IO_2_1_stdout_; _IO_2_1_stderr_;`
  entries from version_scripts/libc.map so the linker no longer exports
  those names from the release ABI.
- Drop the three `sync_copy_relocated_stdio_symbol(c"_IO_2_1_*", ...)`
  calls inside init_host_stdio_streams, since there are no longer
  exported aliases to keep in sync.
- Keep the existing debug-only `IO_2_1_STDIN/STDOUT/STDERR` sentinel
  statics so the unit test surface that pokes those names directly
  still compiles. The release ABI simply does not export them anymore;
  preloaded processes that genuinely need `_IO_2_1_*` storage will get
  it from the host glibc that is still in the link, not from an
  aliased pointer cell in our libc.

Pure ABI cleanup; no functional change to publicly-documented stdio
behaviour.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
